metadata.wedge: BCS first 24 months post-active-treatment, PT senology/oncology hospital follow-up
metadata.chosen_thesis: BT-03 — PT private oncology white-label (CUF / Lusíadas / Luz Saúde)
metadata.ranking_calibration: Ranking biased to BT-03 PoV needs: 2 PT private senology LOIs by week 12, EUR 800–1500/hospital/month, GDPR DPIA co-signed, Class I MDR pathway

entries.name: CUF Oncologia — Centro de Senologia (CUF Descobertas / CUF Tejo)

relevant_program.name: CUF Centros de Senologia (multidisciplinary breast unit, includes psycho-oncology referral)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling — procurement managed at CUF central direction (José de Mello Saúde) plus unit director sign-off
relevant_program.focus: Breast cancer multidisciplinary care, follow-up months 0–24, NPS/quality differentiation vs SNS

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Anchor buyer for BT-03. CUF runs a flagship Centro de Senologia at Descobertas/Tejo with a structured BCS follow-up pathway and a quality director who already reports survivorship metrics; the <20% psych-support gap [EV-016] is exactly their competitive blind spot.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Wins one CUF unit → multiplies across the CUF group (8+ senology-active sites); reference site for BT-02 pharma research-services.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A senology-team dashboard that turns the <20% psych-support gap into a measured, accreditation-ready number — alerts caught vs missed, time-to-intervention, PHQ-9/GAD-7 trajectory — at EUR 800–1500/hospital/month [EV-084], ~5x cheaper than the psycho-oncologist FTE the head cannot hire [EV-019]. DPIA co-signed with their DPO; Class I self-cert; bounded alert budget per clinician contracted as success criterion.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) LOI to host the 8–12-week PoV at one CUF Senology Centre with ≥30 BCS, (b) introduction to CUF central oncology direction for group-wide expansion, (c) DPO co-design of the DPIA, (d) commitment to quality-report co-authorship at week 12.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Warm intro via PerSense's existing iNNOVSensing collaborators; fallback: direct email to CUF Oncologia direction + LinkedIn to head of CUF Senologia

open_issues: Named senology unit director / head of oncology operations not yet identified — must confirm before week 1
Whether procurement decision sits at unit level or at José de Mello Saúde central oncology direction is unverified
No prior signed CUF LOI in the file — pricing anchor is EV-084, not a CUF signature
entries.next_action: This week: identify the head of CUF Senologia at Descobertas/Tejo via LinkedIn + Sociedade Portuguesa de Senologia member directory; draft a 1-page buyer-facing brief framing the <20% gap as their NPS risk.

entries.name: Lusíadas Saúde — Centro Mama (Lusíadas Lisboa / Porto)

relevant_program.name: Lusíadas Centro Mama (breast unit with structured follow-up and psychology link)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling; procurement runs through Lusíadas medical direction with clinical-director endorsement
relevant_program.focus: Breast cancer dx + treatment + follow-up; differentiation on patient experience vs CUF/Luz

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Second of three named BT-03 buyers. Lusíadas explicitly markets the Centro Mama as integrated multidisciplinary care — a survivorship dashboard reporting PHQ-9/GAD-7 trajectory and time-to-intervention is a direct fit for their public differentiation narrative.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: One LOI here = competitive pressure on CUF/Luz and a second reference site that de-risks BT-02 pharma research-services.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A buyer-facing dashboard usable in their patient-experience reporting; the same SaaS price (EUR 800–1500/mo) at ~5x discount to a psycho-oncologist FTE; clinician burden capped as a contractual PoV success criterion (problem.md OI3).
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) LOI to co-host the PoV (single-site or paired with CUF), (b) intro to medical direction, (c) hospital DPO time for DPIA, (d) right to publish anonymised aggregate alert/intervention metrics in the Evidence Notes.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct email to Lusíadas Centro Mama clinical lead + warm intro via Sociedade Portuguesa de Senologia

open_issues: Centro Mama clinical lead name not confirmed
Lusíadas internal procurement policy on SaaS sub-EUR-20K contracts unverified
No prior PerSense relationship at Lusíadas in the ledger
entries.next_action: This week: cold-email Centro Mama clinical lead with a one-paragraph pain statement (the <20% gap, their NPS exposure) and a request for a 30-min intro call — explicitly NOT a pitch.

entries.name: Hospital da Luz — Centro Clínico da Mama (Luz Saúde / Fidelidade)

relevant_program.name: Hospital da Luz Centro Clínico da Mama (multidisciplinary breast care, Lisboa flagship)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling; Luz Saúde innovation programs occasionally run through Fidelidade-side digital-health calls
relevant_program.focus: Breast cancer integrated pathway; Luz Saúde markets innovation/digital differentiation

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Third named BT-03 buyer. Luz Saúde (owned by Fidelidade) has an explicit innovation/digital-health posture, lowering procurement friction for a sub-EUR-20K-ACV SaaS dashboard and creating a natural bridge to BT-05 via Fidelidade.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Fidelidade ownership = potential bridge to insurer co-sponsorship of survivorship benefits (BT-05).

entries.what_we_offer_them: Same dashboard + JITAI sensing layer; PLUS a defensible insurer-readable outcome dataset (alert volume, intervention timeliness, PHQ-9/GAD-7 deltas) that Fidelidade-side stakeholders can use later in benefit-design discussions.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) LOI for PoV at Centro Clínico da Mama, (b) intro to Luz Saúde innovation team and to Fidelidade health stakeholder, (c) DPIA co-design.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct outreach to Luz Saúde innovation team + Centro Clínico da Mama clinical director

open_issues: Luz Saúde innovation program name/URL not verified for an active 2025–2026 cycle that fits a survivorship pilot
Decision rights between hospital-side clinical director and Luz Saúde central innovation are unclear
No PerSense prior contact in ledger
entries.next_action: This week: search Luz Saúde news/press for any 2024–2026 digital-health pilot announcements; if none, go direct to the Centro Clínico da Mama clinical lead.

entries.name: Sociedade Portuguesa de Senologia (SPS)

relevant_program.name: SPS Congresso Nacional de Senologia + Working Groups (psycho-oncology/survivorship subgroup)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Annual congress (typically Q1–Q2); working groups year-round
relevant_program.focus: Portuguese senology professional body — reaches CUF/Lusíadas/Luz/IPO/Champalimaud senologists in one room

fit_to_thesis.rationale: SPS is the multiplication play. Endorsement or a working-group slot puts BT-03 in front of every senologist at every PT private group plus IPO and university hospitals — the entire BT-03 + BT-04 buyer pool — in one outreach motion.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: One SPS endorsement unlocks warm intros across all BT-03 hospital groups + IPO + Champalimaud + university hospitals.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A working-group-ready evidence package on the survivorship psych-support gap (<20% [EV-016], 30–40% [EV-019]) and a co-authorship/co-presentation slot at the next congress on between-consultation detection — PerSense brings the data, SPS brings clinical credibility.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Introduction to the senology heads at CUF/Lusíadas/Luz, (b) endorsement letter or working-group inclusion for the PoV protocol, (c) congress slot for week-12 results presentation.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct contact via SPS board email + warm intro through any current PerSense clinical collaborator who is an SPS member

open_issues: Current SPS board / psycho-oncology working group lead not yet named here
SPS endorsement is reputational, not contractual — must not be over-counted as an LOI
entries.next_action: This week: pull the current SPS board from spsenologia.pt and email the president + closest psycho-oncology working-group lead with a 1-page brief; ask for a 20-min call, not a slot.

entries.name: IPO Lisboa Francisco Gentil — Psico-oncologia & Sobrevivência

relevant_program.name: IPO Lisboa Serviço de Psico-Oncologia + Programa de Sobrevivência
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling clinical research collaborations; FCT and EU-funded calls intermittently
relevant_program.focus: Psycho-oncology and survivorship clinics; reference centre for breast cancer in PT public oncology

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Backup PoV site if private channel does not convert by week 4. IPO has the clinical credibility and patient volume; a public-channel pilot also builds the BT-04 expansion case (EU consortium, Mission Cancer).
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Unlocks BT-04 (EU/FCT-funded consortium) and adds the public-system credibility needed for BT-01 (DiGA) dossier later.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Co-investigator role on a BCS between-consultation detection protocol; PerSense AUC 0.78 [EV-043, internal_only] replicated in a public PT cohort = a publishable result IPO can claim; no paid SaaS pressure.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) LOI as PoV research site if private fallback triggers, (b) co-authorship on the protocol, (c) ethics committee fast-track via existing IPO research office.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct contact with IPO Lisboa Psico-Oncologia service via existing PerSense academic collaborators (iNNOVSensing network)

open_issues: IPO ethics committee timelines are typically 8–16 weeks — may not fit the 8–12-week PoV window
IPO contracts are not BT-03 SaaS revenue; tagging as PoV-site backup, not as a buyer signature
Specific psico-oncologia service head not named here
entries.next_action: This week: identify the current head of IPO Lisboa Psico-Oncologia and email a 1-page research-collaboration framing; in parallel, ask about ethics-committee timelines so the team knows by week 4 whether IPO is realistic as a fallback.

entries.name: IPO Porto — Psico-oncologia / Survivorship Research

relevant_program.name: IPO Porto research collaborations + psico-oncologia clinical service
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling research collaborations
relevant_program.focus: Oncology reference + research; close to ISPUP / U.Porto research network

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Secondary public-channel option; useful as a north-region site if Lisboa-centric private channel runs into ethics delays.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Bridges to ISPUP and U.Porto BT-04 EU-grant capabilities.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Same as IPO Lisboa: research co-investigator role, AUC 0.78 replication on a fresh cohort, publishable outcome.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Research-site LOI if Porto channel is needed; protocol co-authorship.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Warm intro via ISPUP

open_issues: Lisboa-centric pilot logistics make Porto a backup-of-a-backup
No named head of psico-oncologia identified
entries.next_action: Park until week 4; if both private and IPO Lisboa stall, re-activate via ISPUP intro.

entries.name: IPO Coimbra — Psico-oncologia

relevant_program.name: IPO Coimbra clinical research + survivorship program
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling
relevant_program.focus: Central-region oncology reference

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Coverage option if Lisboa+Porto fail; weaker BT-03 fit.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 only.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Research collaboration; AUC 0.78 external replication.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Backup PoV site LOI.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct email

open_issues: Geographic distance from current PerSense team
No prior IPO Coimbra relationship in ledger
entries.next_action: Defer to month 2.

entries.name: Liga Portuguesa Contra o Cancro — Núcleos Regionais & MAMA / Vencer e Viver

relevant_program.name: Movimento Vencer e Viver / Núcleo Regional do Sul (apoio a sobreviventes de cancro da mama)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Continuous community programs; small grants intermittent
relevant_program.focus: Patient-side BCS support, awareness, peer-to-peer survivor networks

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Patient-recruitment amplifier and patient-side validation channel; not a paying buyer for BT-03 but a credibility multiplier and recruitment funnel for the PoV.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Patient advocacy backing supports BT-05 (insurer benefit) and BT-04 (EU public-impact narrative).

entries.what_we_offer_them: Aggregated, consented insight into BCS post-treatment emotional trajectories — the kind of data the Liga has never had, framed for their advocacy reports.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Endorsement of the PoV, (b) recruitment support via Vencer e Viver groups, (c) participation in patient-side advisory.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Núcleo Regional do Sul direct contact + Movimento Vencer e Viver coordinators

open_issues: Current Movimento Vencer e Viver national coordinator not named
Patient-advocacy backing must not be confused with a hospital LOI
entries.next_action: Month 1: contact Núcleo Regional do Sul as a patient-advisory and recruitment partner.

entries.name: CHULN — Hospital de Santa Maria, Centro de Senologia

relevant_program.name: CHULN Senologia + Psico-oncologia (Hospital Santa Maria)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling research collaborations; ethics committee 8–14 weeks
relevant_program.focus: Public-system reference for breast cancer; teaching hospital linked to FMUL

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Public-system PoV-site option with FMUL academic anchor for BT-04. Slower procurement than private; not the BT-03 first-revenue path.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 (EU consortium) and academic publication base.

entries.what_we_offer_them: PT-public reference site for a JITAI BCS protocol; co-authorship; research-grade dataset.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Research-site LOI if needed; ethics committee accelerated review.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Warm intro via NOVA Medical or FMUL PerSense academic contacts

open_issues: Ethics committee timeline incompatible with 8–12-week PoV window unless pre-approval already exists
entries.next_action: Defer; useful if BT-04 grant work activates.

entries.name: Champalimaud Foundation — Breast Unit

relevant_program.name: Champalimaud Breast Unit (clinical care + research)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling research; internal funding cycles via Champalimaud Foundation
relevant_program.focus: Premium BCS care + neuroscience-adjacent research

fit_to_thesis.rationale: High clinical credibility; the Breast Unit's research orientation aligns with PerSense's AUC 0.78 [EV-043] replication ambition. A Champalimaud co-author massively strengthens the BT-01 DiGA dossier and BT-04 EU consortium.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-01 (DiGA dossier credibility), BT-04 (EU grant lead).

entries.what_we_offer_them: Research co-author position on between-consultation BCS detection; publishable AUC replication; access to PerSense iNNOVSensing dataset for joint analysis.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Co-investigator role; protocol review; reference letter for EU grant applications.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct outreach to Breast Unit research lead

open_issues: Champalimaud is research-heavy and may not function as a paying BT-03 buyer
Procurement model unclear — likely grant-funded, not SaaS-funded
entries.next_action: Month 1: contact Breast Unit research lead about a research collaboration on between-consultation detection.

entries.name: NOVA Medical School / CHRC — Comprehensive Health Research Centre

relevant_program.name: CHRC research lines incl. mental health & oncology survivorship
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling; FCT calls + EU Mission Cancer windows
relevant_program.focus: Translational health research at NOVA Medical School

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Academic anchor for BT-04 EU grants and dossier credibility; strong link to CHULN for ethics.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 (EU consortium lead).

entries.what_we_offer_them: Co-PI position on EU grant submissions using PoV data; access to a real-world cohort.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Co-PI commitment; ethics support; methodology review.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct contact via CHRC oncology / mental-health research line leads

open_issues: Specific research-line lead not named
BT-04 timelines (12–18 mo) do not help BT-03 first-revenue
entries.next_action: Month 1–2: align with CHRC for BT-04 EU grant submission window.

entries.name: ISPUP — Instituto de Saúde Pública, Universidade do Porto

relevant_program.name: ISPUP psycho-oncology + EPIPorto cohort research
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling research; FCT cycles
relevant_program.focus: Population-health research; psycho-oncology and survivorship

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Academic depth in psycho-oncology; useful for BT-04 EU consortium.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 (EU grant), BT-01 (DiGA dossier).

entries.what_we_offer_them: Co-author position on between-consultation detection paper; access to a fresh PT cohort for replication of AUC 0.78 [EV-043].
entries.what_we_ask_them: Co-investigator role; methodology peer review; intro to IPO Porto.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct outreach to ISPUP psycho-oncology line

open_issues: Specific psycho-oncology lead not named
entries.next_action: Month 1–2.

entries.name: Roche Portugal — Medical Affairs (Breast Oncology)

relevant_program.name: Roche Portugal Medical Affairs — breast oncology portfolio (Kadcyla, Phesgo, Perjeta)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Investigator-initiated study (IIS) and medical-grant calls run on internal cycles
relevant_program.focus: Breast oncology medical affairs; adherence and patient-experience outcomes

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Roche is the pharma BT-02 anchor: breast-onc lead + interest in survivorship-adherence outcomes. Pre-PoV, this is a relationship-build, not a contract.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-02 (research-services EUR 100–300K/study).

entries.what_we_offer_them: A real-world cohort and an outcome dataset (PHQ-9/GAD-7 trajectory + treatment-adherence proxy) usable in IIS proposals.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Medical Affairs intro call once PoV results land; (b) IIS submission window timing; (c) reference for BT-04 EU grant if applicable.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct outreach to Roche PT Medical Affairs breast-onc lead

open_issues: Pharma procurement is 6–12 mo and dependent on PoV evidence existing first
No PerSense prior Roche contact in ledger
entries.next_action: Month 3 onward, after PoV interim results.

entries.name: AstraZeneca / Novartis Portugal — Oncology Medical Affairs

relevant_program.name: AstraZeneca / Novartis PT Oncology Medical Affairs — IIS / medical grant programs
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Internal IIS cycles
relevant_program.focus: Breast-onc + supportive-care outcomes

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Diversification on BT-02 alongside Roche; breast-onc presence at AZ (PARP inhibitors) and Novartis (CDK4/6) is strong but a step behind Roche's mainline BCS portfolio for survivorship-adherence framing.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Same: real-world adherence + survivorship dataset.
entries.what_we_ask_them: IIS conversation post-PoV.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Direct outreach to Medical Affairs Oncology

open_issues: Less direct breast-onc fit than Roche for BCS-survivorship specifically
Two firms bundled — separate before outreach
entries.next_action: Month 6+; before reaching out, split into one row each.

entries.name: EIT Health Portugal node + RIS Hub / InnoStars

relevant_program.name: EIT Health InnoStars / RIS Innovation Call (PT-eligible)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Annual calls — typically Q1 open, Q2 close; check current cycle
relevant_program.focus: Health-innovation acceleration; matchmaking with hospital partners and EU grants

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Non-dilutive funding + warm intros to other EU hospitals (BT-04 expansion). Not a buyer; a multiplier.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 (EU consortium), BT-01 (DiGA dossier credibility).

entries.what_we_offer_them: A live PoV with PT private-hospital partners and a defensible AUC 0.78 internal benchmark — a fundable story.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Application support for the next InnoStars/RIS cycle, (b) intros to EU breast-cancer survivorship clinicians.

contact_path.preferred_channel: EIT Health PT node liaison + InnoStars programme manager

open_issues: Current cycle deadlines not verified in this brief — unverified deadline
EIT Health funding is non-dilutive but slow vs BT-03 6-mo timeline
entries.next_action: Month 1: confirm current InnoStars/RIS deadlines and PT-node liaison.

entries.name: EU Mission Cancer / Horizon Europe — Survivorship calls

relevant_program.name: Horizon Europe Mission Cancer — survivorship and quality-of-life topics
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Annual work-programme cycles; 2026 calls being scoped
relevant_program.focus: Cancer prevention, treatment, and quality-of-life for survivors

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BT-04 funding line. Requires a multi-country consortium — depends on Champalimaud / NOVA / IPO PT participation + 1–2 EU partners.

entries.what_we_offer_them: PT BCS cohort + JITAI sensing layer + PerSense AUC 0.78 internal benchmark.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Co-applicant slots; consortium-formation introductions.

contact_path.preferred_channel: EU NCP PT (via FCT) + EIT Health match-making

open_issues: EIC Accelerator success rate ~5.9% [EV-063]; Mission Cancer broadly similar — not a near-term cash line
No consortium partners signed
entries.next_action: Month 2–3 once academic anchors (Champalimaud / NOVA / ISPUP) are aligned.

entries.name: Casa do Impacto (SCML) — Health track / MAZE-backed cohort

relevant_program.name: Casa do Impacto incubation / acceleration cohorts (health-impact track)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Annual cohort intake (typically Q1)
relevant_program.focus: Social-impact ventures; SCML backing; MAZE-linked

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BIG Impact named partner. Useful for impact-narrative packaging (BCS social-cost framing) and warm intros to SCML-network hospitals; not directly a BT-03 buyer.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-04 (impact-funding narrative); BIG Impact cohort signal.

entries.what_we_offer_them: An impact story with concrete numbers (BCS treatment gap [EV-016/EV-019], AUC 0.78 detection capability [EV-043], PoV pilot site count) usable in SCML reporting.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Cohort acceptance, (b) intros to SCML-network and MAZE health investors, (c) impact-measurement framework support.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Casa do Impacto direct application + BIG Impact program coordinator

open_issues: Current health-track program details and 2026 cohort cycle not verified — unverified program existence in current cycle
Casa do Impacto support is reputational/intro, not a BT-03 LOI
entries.next_action: Month 1: verify current cohort cycle dates with BIG Impact program coordinator.

entries.name: Nova SBE — Health Economics & Management Knowledge Center

relevant_program.name: Nova SBE Health Economics & Management Knowledge Center
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Rolling research collaborations; executive education cycles
relevant_program.focus: Health economics, hospital management, value-based healthcare

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BIG Impact named partner. Provides the cost-of-care framing that BT-03 buyers (and later BT-05 insurers) will demand: psycho-oncologist-FTE-equivalence math, NPS-impact modelling.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-05 (insurer business case); BT-03 buyer-pitch credibility.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A live PT pilot dataset for hospital-economics analysis; co-authorship on a BCS survivorship cost paper.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Health-economics framework for the buyer pitch, (b) co-authorship on a PT BCS survivorship cost study, (c) intros to CUF/Lusíadas/Luz alumni in management roles.

contact_path.preferred_channel: BIG Impact program coordinator → Nova SBE Health KC

open_issues: Specific Health KC senior researcher not named
Output is academic, not a hospital LOI — must not be over-counted
entries.next_action: Week 1–2: ask BIG Impact coordinator for a Nova SBE Health KC intro to discuss the BCS survivorship cost framing.

entries.name: Fundação Mendes Gonçalves

relevant_program.name: Fundação Mendes Gonçalves social-impact program (BIG Impact partner)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Annual social-impact cycle (BIG Impact)
relevant_program.focus: Social-impact, education, health social initiatives

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BIG Impact named partner. Limited direct BCS-survivorship program fit; primary value is BIG-Impact-cohort signalling and any small-grant matching.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BIG Impact cohort credibility only.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A measurable social-impact metric (BCS women monitored, alerts intercepted, treatment-gap closure %).
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Confirmation of any health-aligned grant cycle, (b) BIG Impact cohort visibility.

contact_path.preferred_channel: BIG Impact program coordinator

open_issues: No verified specific health-funding program at Mendes Gonçalves Foundation surfaced in this brief — unverified program existence for BCS-fit
Likely a generic foundation, not a health-specialised funder
entries.next_action: Week 1–2: ask BIG Impact program coordinator whether Mendes Gonçalves has any health-aligned grant cycle in 2026.

entries.name: Siemens Healthineers Portugal — Innovation

relevant_program.name: Siemens Healthineers innovation challenges / digital-health partnerships (PT operations)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Internal innovation cycles; case-by-case partnerships
relevant_program.focus: Imaging + diagnostic + digital-health; entry into oncology software is exploratory

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BIG Impact named partner. Healthineers' core is imaging, not psycho-oncology DTx; their BCS lens overlaps mainly via mammography workflow integration, which is adjacent — not BT-03's PoV core.
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: Possible long-tail: integration with imaging-driven follow-up workflows in BT-03 hospitals.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Optionality on a survivorship dashboard that complements imaging-driven follow-up at private senology centres.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Innovation-team meeting via BIG Impact, (b) low-effort co-marketing or co-pilot conversation if a CUF/Luz site is shared.

entries.next_action: Defer; revisit only if a BT-03 hospital partner mentions a Healthineers contract overlap.

entries.name: Médis (Ageas) — Programa de Oncologia

relevant_program.name: Médis Programa de Oncologia (case management for oncology beneficiaries)
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Insurer-product cycles (slow); innovation conversations rolling
relevant_program.focus: Oncology benefit management; case-management programs

fit_to_thesis.rationale: BT-05 future. PoV does not produce the cost-of-care delta insurers need (per chosen_thesis BT-05 rationale) — relationship-build only until BT-03 evidence exists.

entries.what_we_offer_them: Future visibility into a survivorship benefit usable in their NPS / oncology-benefit narrative.
entries.what_we_ask_them: Quarterly catch-up while PoV runs.

contact_path.preferred_channel: Médis innovation/partnerships team direct

open_issues: BT-05 explicitly deferred in chosen_thesis
PoV does not produce the actuarial outcome insurers need
entries.next_action: Month 6+.

entries.name: Ordem dos Psicólogos Portugueses (OPP) — Núcleo Psicologia da Saúde / Psico-oncologia

relevant_program.name: OPP Núcleo de Psicologia da Saúde / psico-oncologia
relevant_program.deadline_or_cycle: Continuous; annual psychology congress
relevant_program.focus: Professional regulation and standards for PT psychologists; psico-oncologia subgroup

fit_to_thesis.rationale: Endorsement reduces clinician-side resistance to JITAI alerts and supports the buyer-empathy story (alert volume bounded, psychologist-aligned escalation pathway).
fit_to_thesis.expansion_match: BT-03 buyer-pitch credibility; future BT-05 standard-setting.

entries.what_we_offer_them: A protocol that respects clinician scope-of-practice (alerts trigger clinician-mediated outreach, not automated therapy) + transparent alert-volume budget.
entries.what_we_ask_them: (a) Endorsement letter on the alert-pathway design, (b) intro to the psico-oncologia working group lead.

contact_path.preferred_channel: OPP direct via Núcleo de Psicologia da Saúde

open_issues: Current psico-oncologia subgroup lead not named here
Endorsement is reputational, not contractual
entries.next_action: Month 1–2: contact OPP Núcleo de Psicologia da Saúde.
